claim: Evidence schema, prompt, readiness contract, and tests all distinguish project, external, user, and unknown evidence scopes.

evidence.summary: Explorer and cross-checker both independently verified the evidence schema definition (DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION) and VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES constant that define the four scope types.
evidence.why: Verifies both agents independently confirmed the same schema definition with accurate line citations.

evidence.summary: Both agents verified the scope classification logic (evidenceRecordScope) and validation functions (evidenceRecordHasDeclaredScope, evidenceRecordHasProjectLocator, evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues) that implement scope distinction.
evidence.why: Confirms the implementation logic matches the schema definition across multiple functions.

evidence.summary: Both agents verified projectEvidenceBlockingIssues() function blocks readiness when project facts lack project-scoped evidence.
evidence.why: Verifies the readiness contract enforces scope requirements.

evidence.summary: Both agents confirmed VALID_EVIDENCE_SCOPES constant is consistent across router and evidence-dispatch modules.
evidence.why: Verifies scope validation consistency across the evidence system.

evidence.summary: Cross-checker verified evidenceScopeErrors() function validates each evidence record has a valid scope.
evidence.why: Confirms validation enforcement occurs during normalization.

evidence.summary: Explorer verified buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt() generates agent prompts with explicit scope requirements; cross-checker confirmed the hard rules at lines 271-272.
evidence.why: Verifies agent prompts explicitly instruct scope distinction.

evidence.summary: Cross-checker verified buildBoundarySnapshot() and diffBoundarySnapshots() implement filesystem-based boundary enforcement using SHA-256 digests.
Both agents verified test coverage for scope enforcement: external-only evidence blocking, project locator requirements, URL detection, and scope declaration requirements.
evidence.why: Verifies the acceptance criteria have test coverage proving the behavior.

evidence.summary: Both agents verified test coverage for boundary enforcement: mutation detection, git-backed detection, and non-git fallback.
evidence.why: Verifies boundary system has comprehensive test coverage.

result.notes: Verifier confirms no assumptions are presented as facts: both explorer and cross-checker have empty assumptions arrays. All evidence paths/sources are usable: 18 explorer records and 11 cross-checker records all contain concrete repo-relative paths, accurate line citations, declared scopes, and source fields. No unresolved missing data: both agents returned empty missing arrays. Cross-check confirms explorer findings with no conflicts. All PRD readiness criteria are satisfied with high-confidence evidence from project code and tests.
